# Benjamin James

> Benjamin James is a PhD candidate in computational biology at MIT CSAIL (Manolis Kellis's lab) studying the gene regulatory landscape of human disease using single-cell and spatial omics. He is an NSF Graduate Research Fellow and a Frederick (1953) and Barbara Cronin Fellow.

## Identity

- **Name:** Benjamin James
- **Role:** PhD candidate, Computational Biology
- **Affiliation:** MIT CSAIL, Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (EECS)
- **Email:** benjames@mit.edu

## Research

Benjamin studies computational genomics, with a focus on single-cell and spatial omics techniques to understand gene regulatory mechanisms underlying human disease. He previously developed efficient alignment-free k-mer based methods for sequence homology as an undergraduate at the University of Tulsa under Hani Girgis, graduating with B.S. degrees in Computer Science and Mathematics.

## Selected Publications

- Boix, C.A., **James, B.T.**, Park, Y.P., Meuleman, W., & Kellis, M. (2021). *Regulatory genomic circuitry of human disease loci by integrative epigenomics.*
- **James, B.T.**, Luczak, B.B., & Girgis, H.Z. (2018). *MeShClust: an intelligent tool for clustering DNA sequences.*
- Xiong*, X., **James\***, B.T., Boix*, C.A., et al. (2023). *Epigenomic dissection of Alzheimer's disease pinpoints causal variants and reveals epigenome erosion.* (* co-first author)

Full publications list: see publications.html
